items.title: Jail then deportation for man who reversed over pub patrons

children.content: A New Zealand national has been jailed for six years after he admitted to reversing into a crowd of people at a South Australian pub.

items.title: Public servants asked to remove word 'harmful' about SA algal bloom, FOI reveals

children.content: Emails released under FOI laws and obtained by the SA Liberal Party show government workers were removing the word "harmful" from communications about the algal bloom in SA waters, with one team leader labelling it "bizarre".

items.title: SA's history preservation organisation faces questions over future

items.title: Calls for change after soccer club told it needs men's team to compete

children.content: Millicent United's women's side is flying this season, but without a men's team the club has been told they are unable to compete.

items.title: Migrant delivery driver says assault claim was not taken seriously by SA Police

children.content: A migrant delivery driver who alleges he was assaulted while working in Adelaide's CBD says he was not taken seriously by SA Police, who told him the case had been closed before he had given a written statement.

items.title: Thousands travel to outback Agfair despite fuel costs

children.content: Fuel costs have not stopped thousands of people from descending upon Broken Hill for the biannual Agfair.

items.title: Police confirm identity of remains found in SA bushland

children.content: SA Police say remains found in bushland south of Adelaide a week ago have been identified as belonging to Jeff Mundy, who was last seen by his family five years ago.

items.title: The alternative classroom disengaged students are choosing to be in

items.title: SA government needs certainty on LIV Golf by October, premier says

items.title: Remembering SA's pain and battles behind the Murray-Darling Basin Plan

children.content: The Murray-Darling Basin Plan is under review, but with submissions due to close today, we take a step back in time to remember when dried lake beds were turning acidic, dairy farmers were selling up, and there was talk of water carting for Adelaide.

items.title: Australian businesses falling victim to new type of fraud

children.content: Higher inflation, a war in the Middle East, and a ban on surcharges are crippling small businesses. Now they are falling victim to a modern crime.

items.title: Daughter hopes $1m reward will solve mystery of mother's disappearance

items.title: Russian woman's husband beat her but police were no help, so she wrote to Putin

items.title: It was a record race time. Moments later spectators were suspicious

items.title: Three die after suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
